question_text: The compound that does NOT liberate CO2, on treatment with aqueous sodium bicarbonate solution, is (A) Benzoic acid (B) Benzenesulphonic acid (C) Salicylic acid (D) Carbolic acid (Phenol)

KI in acetone, undergoes SN2 reaction with each of P (CH3Cl), Q (tertiary alkyl chloride), R (allyl chloride) and S (phenacyl chloride/benzyl). The rates of the reaction vary as (A) P>Q>R>S (B) S>P>R>Q (C) P>R>Q>S (D) R>P>S>Q

Among P, Q, R and S, the aromatic compound(s) is/are: cyclopropyl chloride+AlCl3-->P; cyclopentadiene+NaH-->Q; dimedone-type diketone+(NH4)2CO3 at 100-115 C-->R; benzaldehyde+HCl-->S. (A) P (B) Q (C) R (D) S

The hyperconjugative stabilities of tert-butyl cation and 2-butene, respectively, are due to (A) sigma->p(empty) and sigma->pi* electron delocalisations (B) sigma->sigma* and sigma->pi electron delocalisations (C) sigma->p(filled) and sigma->pi electron delocalisations (D) p(filled)->sigma* and sigma->pi* electron delocalisations
The total number of carboxylic acid groups in the product P is [cyclohexene-fused anhydride treated with: 1. H3O+/delta, 2. O3, 3. H2O2 --> P]

A tetrapeptide has -COOH group on alanine. This produces glycine (Gly), valine (Val), phenylalanine (Phe) and alanine (Ala), on complete hydrolysis. For this tetrapeptide, the number of possible sequences (primary structures) with -NH2 group attached to a chiral center is

In the following reaction, the product(s) formed is(are): 4-methylphenol + CHCl3/OH- --> ? Showing products P (2,6-diformyl-4-methylphenol), Q (4-methyl-4-(dichloromethyl)cyclohexa-2,5-dien-1-one), R (similar dienone), S (2-formyl-4-methylphenol). (A) P (major) (B) Q (minor) (C) R (minor) (D) S (major)

The major product(s) of the following reaction is(are): 4-hydroxybenzene-1-sulfonic acid + aqueous Br2 (3.0 equivalents) --> ? Showing products P (2,6-dibromo-4-sulfophenol with Br), Q (tribromophenol), R (2,4,6-tribromophenol), S (2-bromo-4-sulfophenol with extra Br).

After completion of the reactions (I and II), the organic compound(s) in the reaction mixtures is(are): Reaction I: acetone (1.0 mol) + Br2 (1.0 mol) in aqueous NaOH. Reaction II: acetone (1.0 mol) + Br2 (1.0 mol) in CH3COOH. Products shown: P (BrCH2COCH3), Q (CBr3COCH3), R (Br3CCOCH2Br), S (BrCH2COCH2Br), T (CH3COONa), U (CHBr3).

P and Q are isomers of dicarboxylic acid C4H4O4. Both decolorize Br2/H2O. On heating, P forms the cyclic anhydride. Upon treatment with dilute alkaline KMnO4, P as well as Q could produce one or more than one from S (meso-tartaric acid), T, and U (tartaric acid stereoisomers). Compounds formed from P and Q are, respectively (A) Optically active S and optically active pair (T,U) (B) Optically inactive S and optically inactive pair (T,U) (C) Optically active pair (T,U) and optically active S (D) Optically inactive pair (T,U) and optically inactive S

In the following reaction sequences V and W are, respectively: Q (isomer of C4H4O4) --H2/Ni/delta--> V; benzene + V --AlCl3(anhydrous)--> [intermediate] --1.Zn-Hg/HCl, 2.H3PO4--> W. Showing V as succinic anhydride and W as 1,2,3,4-tetrahydronaphthalenone (beta-tetralone), among other options.

Match the chemical conversions in List I with the appropriate reagents in List II: P. tertiary alkyl chloride --> alkene; Q. sodium alkoxide --> diethyl ether; R. cyclopentene --> cyclopentan-1-ol (anti-Markovnikov); S. cyclopentene --> cyclopentan-1-ol (Markovnikov). Reagents: 1. Hg(OAc)2/NaBH4; 2. NaOEt; 3. Et-Br; 4. BH3 then H2O2/NaOH.

The correct combination of names for isomeric alcohols with molecular formula C4H10O is/are (A) tert-butanol and 2-methylpropan-2-ol (B) tert-butanol and 1,1-dimethylethan-1-ol (C) n-butanol and butan-1-ol (D) isobutyl alcohol and 2-methylpropan-1-ol

The reactivity of compound Z (3-tert-butylphenol) with different halogens under appropriate conditions gives mono halo with I2, di halo with Br2, tri halo with Cl2. The observed pattern of electrophilic substitution can be explained by (A) steric effect of halogen (B) steric effect of tert-butyl group (C) electronic effect of phenolic group (D) electronic effect of tert-butyl group

In the reaction shown below, the major product(s) formed is/are: 3-aminobenzamide reacted with acetic anhydride in CH2Cl2. The major product(s) formed is/are [options show mono- and di-acetylated products with free NH2 or NHCOCH3 groups]

Consider all possible isomeric ketones, including stereoisomers, of MW = 100. All these isomers are independently reacted with NaBH4 (NOTE: stereoisomers are also reacted separately). The total number of ketones that give a racemic product(s) is/are

The total number of distinct naturally occurring amino acids obtained by complete acidic hydrolysis of the peptide shown below is [peptide structure with piperazine-dione rings and Aib residues]

The acidic hydrolysis of ether (X) shown below [triphenylmethyl ether: (C6H5)3C-OR + acid -> (C6H5)3C-OH + ROH] is fastest when (A) one phenyl group is replaced by a methyl group (B) one phenyl group is replaced by a para-methoxyphenyl group (C) two phenyl groups are replaced by two para-methoxyphenyl groups (D) no structural change is made to X

Isomers of hexane, based on their branching, can be divided into three distinct classes as shown in the figure. Class I [2,2-dimethylbutane and 2,3-dimethylbutane], Class II [2-methylpentane and 3-methylpentane], Class III [n-hexane]. The correct order of their boiling point is (A) I>II>III (B) III>II>I (C) II>III>I (D) III>I>II

The major product in the following reaction is: Cl(CH2)3COCH3 + 1. CH3MgBr (dry ether, 0 degrees C), 2. aq. acid. Options show: (A) methyl ketone, (B) allylic alcohol with gem-dimethyl, (C) methylenetetrahydrofuran, (D) 2-methyl-2-methyltetrahydrofuran

For the identification of beta-naphthol using dye test, it is necessary to use (A) dichloromethane solution of beta-naphthol (B) acidic solution of beta-naphthol (C) neutral solution of beta-naphthol (D) alkaline solution of beta-naphthol

Schemes 1 and 2 describe sequential transformation of alkynes M and N. Scheme 1: alkyne M (HO-CH2-C≡H) + 1. NaNH2 (excess), 2. CH3CH2I (1 eq), 3. CH3I (1 eq), 4. H2/Lindlar's catalyst -> X. The product X is [options show cis and trans alkenes with methoxy/ethoxy groups]

Scheme 2: alkyne N (hex-1-yn-1-yl group C≡H) + 1. NaNH2 (2 equiv), 2. Br-CH2CH2-OH, 3. H3O+ (mild), 4. H2/Pd/C, 5. CrO3 -> Y. The correct statement with respect to product Y is (A) positive Tollens test, functional isomer of X (B) positive Tollens test, geometrical isomer of X (C) positive iodoform test, functional isomer of X (D) positive iodoform test, geometrical isomer of X

Different possible thermal decomposition pathways for peroxyesters are shown below. Match each pathway (P: -CO2, R'+R'O'; Q: -CO2, R'+R'O'->R'+X'+carbonyl; R: RCO2'+R'O'->-CO2->R'+X'; S: RCO2'+R'O'->-CO2->R'+R'O') from List I with appropriate peroxyester structure from List II.

Match the four starting materials (P: H-C≡C-H, Q: 1,3-dihydroxybenzene, R: nitrobenzene, S: 4-nitrotoluene) given in List I with the corresponding reaction schemes (I-IV leading to C7H6N2O3, C6H6N2O2, C6H5NO3, C6H5NO4) provided in List II.

On strong heating Q gave S, which on further treatment with ethyl 2-bromopropanoate in the presence of KOH followed by acidification, gave compound T. The compound T is (A) glycine (B) alanine (C) valine (D) serine

For the following compounds, the correct statement(s) with respect to nucleophilic substitution reactions is(are) [I: benzyl bromide (phenylmethyl bromide); II: cyclohexyl bromide (bromocyclohexane); III: 2-bromo-2-methylpropane (tert-butyl bromide); IV: (1-bromoethyl)benzene (1-phenylethyl bromide)] [A] I and III follow SN1 mechanism [B] I and II follow SN2 mechanism [C] Compound IV undergoes inversion of configuration [D] Order of reactivity for I, III and IV is: IV>I>III

Compounds P and R upon ozonolysis produce Q and S, respectively. The molecular formula of Q and S is C8H8O. Q undergoes Cannizzaro reaction but not haloform reaction, whereas S undergoes haloform reaction but not Cannizzaro reaction. (i) P + O3/CH2Cl2 then Zn/H2O gives Q (C8H8O); (ii) R + O3/CH2Cl2 then Zn/H2O gives S (C8H8O). The option(s) with suitable combination of P and R is(are)

Q on treatment with H2SO4 at 0°C gives R. The reaction of R with CH3COCl in the presence of anhydrous AlCl3 in CH2Cl2 followed by treatment with H2O produces compound S. The product S is

The reaction of compound P [4-(1,1-dimethylethyl)phenylacetic acid ethyl ester] with CH3MgBr (excess) then H2O gives Q. Q with H2SO4 at 0°C gives R. R with CH3COCl/anhydrous AlCl3/CH2Cl2 then H2O gives S. The reactions, Q to R and R to S, are [A] Dehydration and Friedel-Crafts acylation [B] Aromatic sulfonation and Friedel-Crafts acylation [C] Friedel-Crafts alkylation, dehydration and Friedel-Crafts acylation [D] Friedel-Crafts alkylation and Friedel-Crafts acylation

Product shown is (1S,2S)-1-methyl-2-azidocyclopentane (wedge bond at N3). Options: methylcyclopentanol stereoisomers.

sub_topic: Named Reactions / Hofmann / Cannizzaro / Reaction Chain
question_text: In the following reaction sequence, the amount of D (in g) formed from 10 moles of acetophenone is ___. (H=1, C=12, N=14, O=16, Br=80; yield% in parentheses): PhCOCH3 →(NaOBr/H3O+, 60%)→ A →(NH3,heat, 50%)→ B →(Br2/KOH, 50%)→ C →(Br2 3equiv/AcOH, 100%)→ D

The desired product X (2-hydroxy-2-methyl-2,3-diphenylpropanoic acid, i.e. Ph2C(OH)-C(Me)(Ph)-COOH) can be prepared by reacting the major product of the reactions in LIST-I with one or more appropriate reagents in LIST-II (given: order of migratory aptitude aryl > alkyl > hydrogen). Match LIST-I reactions with LIST-II reagents.

sub_topic: Reaction Mechanisms / SN1 SN2 E1 E2 / Ethers / Halides
question_text: LIST-I contains reactions and LIST-II contains major products. P. (CH3)3CONa + (CH3)3CBr →; Q. (CH3)3COMe + HBr →; R. (CH3)3CBr + NaOMe →; S. (CH3)3CONa + MeBr →. LIST-II: 1.(CH3)3COH; 2.(CH3)3CBr; 3.(CH3)3COMe; 4.CH2=C(CH3)2; 5.(CH3)3CO-C(CH3)3. Match each reaction in LIST-I with one or more products in LIST-II.

The correct order of acid strength of the following carboxylic acids is [I: propynoic acid (HC≡C-COOH), II: (Z)-prop-2-enoic acid (acrylic acid), III: 4-methoxybenzoic acid (MeO-C6H4-COOH), IV: propanoic acid (CH3CH2COOH)] (A) III>II>I>IV (B) I>II>III>IV (C) I>III>II>IV (D) II>I>IV>III

Which of the following statement(s) is(are) true? (A) Monosaccharides cannot be hydrolysed to give polyhydroxy aldehydes and ketones (B) Oxidation of glucose with bromine water gives glutamic acid (C) Hydrolysis of sucrose gives dextrorotatory glucose and laevorotatory fructose (D) The two six-membered cyclic hemiacetal forms of D-(+)-glucose are called anomers

[Structures of S and T/U are shown as cyclohexane derivatives with methyl and halogen substituents]

Newman projections P, Q, R and S are shown below. P: front carbon has HO, CH3, CH3; back carbon has H3C, C2H5, CH3. Q: front carbon has H3C, OH, CH3; back carbon has H, H, C2H5, CH3. R: front carbon has C2H5, H, CH3; back carbon has HO, CH3, C2H5. S: front carbon has C2H5, CH(CH3)2, CH3; back carbon has H, H, OH. Which one of the following options represents identical molecules?

With respect to the compounds I-V (I: triphenylmethane, II: toluene, III: methane, IV: cyclopentadiene, V: acetylene), choose the correct statement(s). (A) The acidity of compound I is due to delocalization in the conjugate base. (B) The conjugate base of compound IV is aromatic.

In the reaction scheme shown below, Q, R, and S are the major products. P (1,1,4,4-tetramethyl-1,2,3,4-tetrahydronaphthalene derivative) + succinic anhydride / AlCl3 -> Q; Q + (i) Zn-Hg/HCl (ii) H3PO4 -> R; R + (i) CH3MgBr (ii) H3O+ (iii) H2SO4/Delta -> S. The correct structure of...

In the following reaction, compound Q is obtained from compound P via an ionic intermediate. P is 1,1,2-triphenylprop-1-ene-3-yl methanoate (structure: C6H5 groups on C1, C2 with CO2CH3 on C1, C6H5 on C2). P reacted with conc. H2SO4 to give Q (a colored compound). What is the degree of unsaturation of Q?

The structure of a peptide is given below (tyrosine-aspartate-lysine tripeptide with HO-C6H4-CH2 group, CO2H, NH2 groups). If the absolute values of the net charge of the peptide at pH=2, pH=6, and pH=11 are |z1|, |z2|, and |z3|, respectively, then what is |z1| + |z2| + |z3|?
An organic compound (C8H10O2) rotates plane-polarized light. It produces pink color with neutral FeCl3 solution. What is the total number of all the possible isomers for this compound?

Which of the following plots is(are) correct for the given reaction? P (2-bromo-2-methylpropane, i.e., tert-butyl bromide) + NaOH -> Q (2-methylpropan-2-ol) + NaBr. [P]0 is the initial concentration of P. Plots showing t1/2 vs [P]0, Initial rate vs [P]0, [Q]/[P]0 vs time, ln([P]/[P]0) vs time.

Consider the following four compounds I, II, III, and IV. I: aniline (aminobenzene), II: N,N-dimethylaniline, III: 2,4,6-trinitroaniline, IV: N,N-dimethyl-2,4,6-trinitroaniline. Choose the correct statement(s). (A) The order of basicity is II > I > III > IV. (B) The magnitude of pKb difference between I and II is more than that between III and IV.

Consider the following transformations of a compound P. R (optically active) + (i) NaNH2 (ii) C6H5COCH3 (iii) H3O+/Delta -> P (C6H12); P + X(reagent) + (i) KMnO4/H2SO4/Delta -> Q (C8H12O6, optically active acid); P + Pt/H2 -> methylcyclohexane. Choose the correct option(s). (A) P is 1-ethynylcyclohex-1-ene. (B) X is Pd-C/quinoline/H2. (C) P is ethynylcyclohexane. (D) R is shown.

Consider the reaction sequence from P to Q shown below. The overall yield of the major product Q from P is 75%. What is the amount in grams of Q obtained from 9.3 mL of P? P (aniline) + (i) NaNO2 + HCl/0-5 C -> (ii) beta-naphthol + NaOH (iii) CH3CO2H/H2O -> Q. (Use density of P = 1.00 g mL^-1; Molar mass of C = 12.0, H = 1.0, O = 16.0, N = 14.0 g mol^-1)

The major product formed in the following reaction is [starting material: a non-conjugated diyne with triple bonds at C1 and C6 of a 10-carbon chain, reacted with NaNH2, Na, liq. NH3 (Birch-type conditions)]. Options show various products with alkene/alkyne combinations.

Among the following, the conformation that corresponds to the most stable conformation of meso-butane-2,3-diol is [options show Newman projections (A) H, OH, Me anti to Me, H, OH; (B) H, OH, OH gauche to Me, Me, H; (C) Me, OH, OH gauche; (D) H, OH, OH anti arrangement].

For the following reaction scheme, percentage yields are given along the arrow: Mg2C3 -H2O-> P (4.0g) -NaNH2/MeI/75%-> Q -red hot iron tube 873K/40%-> R (x g); also P -Hg2+/H+/333K/100%-> S -Ba(OH)2/heat/80%-> T -NaOCl/80%-> U (y g). The value of x is ___.

For the following reaction scheme (same stem as Q5): Mg2C3 -H2O-> P (4.0g) -NaNH2/MeI/75%-> Q -red hot iron tube 873K/40%-> R (x g); also P -Hg2+/H+/333K/100%-> S -Ba(OH)2/heat/80%-> T -NaOCl/80%-> U (y g). The value of y is ___.

Given: D-Glucose (CHO, H-OH, HO-H, H-OH, H-OH, CH2OH) + HNO3 → P [α]D = +52.7°. The compound(s) which on reaction with HNO3 will give the product having degree of rotation [α]D = -52.7° is(are) [options show four different aldopentose/aldohexose Fischer projections].

The reaction of Q with PhSNa yields an organic compound (major product) that gives positive Carius test on treatment with Na2O2 followed by addition of BaCl2. The correct option(s) for Q is(are) [options: (A) 2,4-dinitrofluorobenzene; (B) 2,4-diiodonitrobenzene; (C) 4-bromo-2-nitro-methylthiobenzene; (D) 2-nitro-4-chloro-methylthiobenzene].
The maximum number of possible isomers (including stereoisomers) which may be formed on mono-bromination of 1-methylcyclohex-1-ene using Br2 and UV light is ___.

In the reaction given below, the total number of atoms having sp2 hybridization in the major product P is ___. [Starting material: bicyclo[4.3.0]non-1(6)-ene (indane-like bicyclic structure with double bond) treated with 1. O3 (excess) then Zn/H2O; 2. NH2OH (excess) → P].

The reaction sequence(s) that would lead to o-xylene as the major product is(are): (A) 2-methylaniline→NaNO2/HCl 273K→CuCN→DIBAL-H then H3O+→N2H4,KOH heat; (B) 2-methylbromobenzene→1.Mg,CO2,H3O+ 2.SOCl2 3.H2,Pd-BaSO4 4.Zn-Hg,HCl; (C) 2-methylstyrene→1.i.BH3 ii.H2O2,NaOH 2.PBr3 3.Zn,dil.HCl; (D) naphthalene→1.O3,Zn/H2O 2.N2H4,KOH,heat.

sub_topic: Reaction Mechanism / Amines / Nitrile / Named Reactions
question_text: Correct option(s) for the following sequence of reactions is(are): PhCH3 -Br2/light-> P; PhCH3 -i.KMnO4,KOH,heat ii.H3O+-> T -1.NH3 2.heat-> U -W-> (branch); P -1.Q 2.H2,Pd/C-> R -CHCl3/KOH-> S (foul smelling); R -V-> (branch). Options: (A) Q=KNO2, W=LiAlH4; (B) R=benzenamine, V=KCN; (C) Q=AgNO2, R=phenylmethanamine; (D) W=LiAlH4, V=AgCN.

Correct match of the C-H bonds (shown in bold) in Column J with their BDE in Column K is: Column J: (P) H-CH(CH3)2; (Q) H-CH2Ph; (R) H-CH=CH2; (S) H-C≡CH. Column K (BDE kcal/mol): (i) 132, (ii) 110, (iii) 95, (iv) 88. Options: (A) P-iii,Q-iv,R-ii,S-i; (B) P-i,Q-ii,R-iii,S-iv; (C) P-iii,Q-ii,R-i,S-iv; (D) P-ii,Q-i,R-iv,S-iii.

For the following reaction CH4(g) + Cl2(g) -light-> CH3Cl(g) + HCl(g), the correct statement is: (A) Initiation step is exothermic with ΔH° = -58 kcal/mol; (B) Propagation step involving CH3 radical formation is exothermic with ΔH° = -2 kcal/mol; (C) Propagation step involving CH3Cl formation is endothermic with ΔH° = +27 kcal/mol; (D) The reaction is exothermic with ΔH° = -25 kcal/mol.

Consider the following reaction: 4-bromobenzyl alcohol (4-Br-C6H4-CH2OH) + red phosphorous/Br2 → R (major product). On estimation of bromine in 1.00 g of R using Carius method, the amount of AgBr formed (in g) is ______. [Given: Atomic mass of H=1, C=12, O=16, P=31, Br=80, Ag=108]

The weight percentage of hydrogen in Q, formed in the following reaction sequence, is ______. Chlorobenzene → (1. NaOH, 623 K, 300 atm; 2. conc. H2SO4 and then conc. HNO3) → Q (major product). [Given: Atomic mass of H=1, C=12, N=14, O=16, S=32, Cl=35]

If the reaction sequence given below is carried out with 15 moles of acetylene, the amount of the product D formed (in g) is ______. HC≡CH → (iron tube, red hot, 80%) A → (H3C-CH2Cl/AlCl3, 50%) B → (1.O2, 2.H3O+, -CH3COCH3, 50%) C → (CH3COCl/pyridine, 100%) D. [Given: Atomic mass of H=1, C=12, O=16, Cl=35]

Considering the reaction sequence: CH3COOH + (1. Br2/red phosphorous, 2. H2O) → P + (phthalimide potassium salt, NaOH, H3O+) → Q + phthalic acid. Correct statement(s): (A) P can be reduced to a primary alcohol using NaBH4. (B) Treating P with conc. NH4OH solution followed by acidification gives Q. (C) Treating Q with NaNO2 in aq. HCl liberates N2. (D) P is more acidic than CH3CH2COOH.

Considering the reaction sequence: 4-nitrotoluene (H3C-C6H4-NO2) → P → Q → R → S (H2O) → T; R also gives via U: benzoic acid. The correct option(s) is(are) regarding P, Q, R, S, T, U in this multi-step synthesis involving reduction, diazotization, and oxidation.

Match the compounds in LIST-I with the observations in LIST-II: (I) Aniline, (II) o-Cresol, (III) Cysteine, (IV) Caprolactam with observations: (P) Sodium fusion extract + FeSO4 then H2SO4 → Prussian blue; (Q) Sodium fusion extract + sodium nitroprusside → blood red; (R) Addition to NaHCO3 → effervescence; (S) Reacts with bromine water → white precipitate; (T) Neutral FeCl3 → violet color.

The number of isomeric tetraenes (NOT containing sp-hybridized carbon atoms) that can be formed from the following reaction sequence is ______. Cyclohex-1-en-3-yne + (1. Na, liquid NH3; 2. Br2 excess; 3. alc. KOH) →
The number of -CH2- (methylene) groups in the product formed from the following reaction sequence is ______. (Z)-hex-2-ene + (1. O3/Zn/H2O; 2. KMnO4; 3. NaOH, electrolysis; 4. Cr2O3, 770 K, 20 atm) →

The total number of chiral molecules formed from one molecule of P on complete ozonolysis (O3, Zn/H2O) is ______. [P is a symmetric polyol with structure: (E)-1-((2R,3S)-3-((S)-1-hydroxy-2-methylprop-2-en-1-yl)-2-methyloxiran... depicted as a complex diol with multiple stereocenters and alkene groups]

Considering the following reaction sequence: benzene + succinic anhydride/AlCl3 → P; P + Zn/Hg, HCl → Q; Q + SOCl2 → R; R + AlCl3 → S; S + Zn/Hg, HCl → a hydrocarbon. The correct statement(s) is(are): (A) P and Q are carboxylic acids. (B) S decolorizes bromine water. (C) P and S react with hydroxylamine to give corresponding oximes. (D) R reacts with dialkylcadmium to give corresponding tertiary alcohol.

Among the following, the correct statement(s) about polymers is(are): (A) The polymerization of chloroprene gives natural rubber. (B) Teflon is prepared from tetrafluoroethene by heating it with persulphate catalyst at high pressures. (C) PVC are thermoplastic polymers. (D) Ethene at 350-570 K temperature and 1000-2000 atm pressure in the presence of a peroxide initiator yields high density polythene.

Treatment of D-glucose with aqueous NaOH results in a mixture of monosaccharides. Which of the following option shows the correct mixture? (Options A-D show Fischer projections of different hexose sugars representing the Lobry de Bruyn-Van Ekenstein rearrangement products.)

In the following reactions, P, Q, R, and S are the major products. CH3CH2CH(CH3)CH2CN reacted with (i) PhMgBr then H3O+ (ii) PhMgBr then H2O gives P. Ph-H + CH3CCl=O reacted with (i) anhyd. AlCl3 (ii) PhMgBr then H2O gives Q. CH3CH2CCl=O reacted with (i) 1/2(PhCH2)2Cd (ii) PhMgBr then H2O gives R. PhCH2CHO reacted with (i) PhMgBr then H2O (ii) CrO3 dil H2SO4 (iii) HCN (iv) H2SO4 delta gives S. The correct statement(s) about P, Q, R, and S is(are)

Consider the following reaction scheme and choose the correct option(s) for the major products Q, R and S. Styrene reacted with (i) B2H6 (ii) NaOH, H2O2, H2O gives P. P reacted with (i) CrO3, H2SO4 (ii) Cl2, Red phosphorus (iii) H2O gives Q. P reacted with (i) SOCl2 (ii) NaCN (iii) H3O+, delta gives R. R reacted with conc. H2SO4 gives S.

In the given reaction scheme, P is a phenyl alkyl ether, Q is an aromatic compound; R and S are the major products. P reacted with HI gives Q. Q reacted with (i) NaOH (ii) CO2 (iii) H3O+ gives R. R reacted with (i) (CH3CO)2O (ii) H3O+ gives S. The correct statement about S is (A) It primarily inhibits noradrenaline degrading enzymes. (B) It inhibits the synthesis of prostaglandin. (C) It is a narcotic drug. (D) It is ortho-acetylbenzoic acid.

The total number of sp2 hybridised carbon atoms in the major product P (a non-heterocyclic compound) of the following reaction is ___. Succinonitrile (NC-CH-CH-CN with two CN groups on adjacent carbons) reacted with (i) LiAlH4 (excess), then H2O (ii) Acetophenone (excess) gives P.

Match the reactions in List-I with the features of their products in List-II. List-I: (P) (-)-1-Bromo-2-ethylpentane (single enantiomer) aq. NaOH SN2; (Q) (-)-2-Bromopentane (single enantiomer) aq. NaOH SN2; (R) (-)-3-Bromo-3-methylhexane (single enantiomer) aq. NaOH SN1; (S) Me-H-Me-Br (single enantiomer) aq. NaOH SN1. List-II: (1) Inversion of configuration (2) Retention of configuration (3) Mixture of enantiomers (4) Mixture of structural isomers (5) Mixture of diastereomers.

The major products obtained from the reactions in List-II are the reactants for the named reactions mentioned in List-I. Match List-I with List-II. List-I: (P) Etard reaction, (Q) Gattermann reaction, (R) Gattermann-Koch reaction, (S) Rosenmund reduction. List-II: (1) Acetophenone + Zn-Hg, HCl, (2) Toluene + (i) KMnO4, KOH, delta (ii) SOCl2, (3) Benzene + CH3Cl + anhyd. AlCl3, (4) Aniline + NaNO2/HCl 273-278K, (5) Phenol + Zn, delta.

In the following reactions, P, Q, R, and S are the major products. 2-methylbutyl chloride reacted with (i) Mg, dry ether (ii) H2O gives P. 2-methylbutyl chloride reacted with (i) Mg, dry ether (ii) CO2, dry ether (iii) H3O+ (iv) NaOH gives Q. 2-methylbutyl chloride reacted with (i) Mg, dry ether (ii) CH3CHO, then H2O (iii) CrO3 gives R. 2-methylbutyl chloride reacted with (i) ethanolic NaCN (ii) H2/Ni (iii) CHCl3/KOH, delta (iv) LiAlH4, then H2O gives S. The correct statement about P, Q, R, and S is

A disaccharide X cannot be oxidised by bromine water. The acid hydrolysis of X leads to a laevorotatory solution. The disaccharide X is [four options showing structural diagrams of different disaccharides with pyranose ring structures]

sub_topic: Aromatic Compounds / Carboxylic Acids / Named Reactions
question_text: In the following reactions, P, Q, R, and S are the major products. 4-ethyl-1-tert-butylbenzene reacted with (i) KMnO4, KOH, delta (ii) H3O+ gives P. MeOOC-phenyl-COCl reacted with (i) NaOH, H2O (ii) H3O+ gives Q. Phenyl compound with C=O and COOMe and CN reacted with (i) H3O+, delta (ii) H2CrO4 gives R. 4-bromo-1,3-dioxolane-2-yl-benzene reacted with (i) Mg, dry ether (ii) CO2, then H3O+ (iii) Ammoniacal AgNO3, H3O+ gives S. The correct statement(s) about P, Q, R, and S is(are)

The reaction of 4-methyloct-1-ene (P, 2.52 g) with HBr in the presence of (C6H5CO)2O2 gives two isomeric bromides in a 9:1 ratio, with a combined yield of 50%. Of these, the entire amount of the primary alkyl bromide was reacted with an appropriate amount of diethylamine followed by treatment with aq. K2CO3 to give a non-ionic product S in 100% yield. The mass (in mg) of S obtained is ___. [Use molar mass (in g mol^-1): H = 1, C = 12, N = 14, Br = 80]

A trinitro compound, 1,3,5-tris(4-nitrophenyl)benzene, on complete reaction with an excess of Sn/HCl gives a major product, which on treatment with an excess of NaNO2/HCl at 0°C provides P as the product. P, upon treatment with excess of H2O at room temperature, gives the product Q. Bromination of Q in aqueous medium furnishes the product R. The number of heteroatoms present in one molecule of R is ___. [Use molar mass: H=1, C=12, N=14, O=16, Br=80, Cl=35.5; atoms other than C and H are heteroatoms]

sub_topic: Aromatic Amines / Diazonium Chemistry / Named Reactions
question_text: A trinitro compound, 1,3,5-tris(4-nitrophenyl)benzene, on complete reaction with an excess of Sn/HCl gives a major product, which on treatment with an excess of NaNO2/HCl at 0°C provides P. P upon treatment with excess of H2O gives Q. Bromination of Q gives R. P upon treatment with an excess of phenol under basic conditions gives S. The total number of carbon atoms and heteroatoms present in one molecule of S is ___.
Aspartame, an artificial sweetener, is a dipeptide aspartyl phenylalanine methyl ester. The structure of aspartame is (structures of phenylalanine and aspartic acid given)

Reaction of iso-propylbenzene with O2 followed by treatment with H3O+ forms phenol and a by-product P. Reaction of P with 3 equivalents of Cl2 gives compound Q. Treatment of Q with Ca(OH)2 produces compound R and calcium salt S. The correct statement(s) regarding P, Q, R and S is(are)

Complete reaction of acetaldehyde with excess formaldehyde, upon heating with conc. NaOH solution, gives P and Q. Compound P does not give Tollens' test, whereas Q on acidification gives positive Tollens' test. Treatment of P with excess cyclohexanone in the presence of catalytic amount of p-toluenesulfonic acid (PTSA) gives product R. Sum of the number of methylene groups (-CH2-) and oxygen atoms in R is ___.

In the following reaction sequence: H-C≡C-CO2Et (C15 chain) treated with i) Hg2+, H3O+; ii) Zn-Hg/HCl; iii) H3O+, heat gives major product P. Glycerol reacts completely with excess P in presence of acid catalyst to form Q. Reaction of Q with excess NaOH followed by CaCl2 yields Ca-soap R quantitatively. Starting with one mole of Q, the amount of R produced in gram is ___.

List-I contains various reaction sequences (ozonolysis, hydroboration, ethylene glycol protection, NaBH4 on cyclic alkenes/ketones) and List-II contains possible diol products. Match each entry in List-I with the appropriate entry in List-II.

sub_topic: Aromatic Compounds / Named Reactions (Diazotization, Nitration, Sulfonation)
question_text: List-I contains various reaction sequences on aromatic substrates (benzenesulfonic acid, nitrobenzene, resorcinol, toluene with KMnO4/nitration/diazotization) and List-II contains different phenolic products (nitrophenols, dinitrophenols). Match each entry in List-I with the appropriate entry in List-II.

sub_topic: Halogenated Compounds / Reaction Sequences (Glucose to Benzene to Chlorobenzene)
question_text: In the following reaction sequence, the major product Q is: L-Glucose → (i) HI, heat; ii) Cr2O3, 775K, 10-20 atm) → P → (Cl2 excess, UV) → Q. Options show various chlorobenzene derivatives (hexachlorobenzene, 1,2,4,5-tetrachlorobenzene, 1,4-dichlorobenzene, hexachlorocyclohexane).

sub_topic: Alkynes / Reaction Sequences (Lindlar Reduction, Imine Reduction)
question_text: The option(s) with correct sequence of reagents for the conversion of P to Q is(are): P is a bicyclic compound with alkyne, CO2Et, CN and acetate groups; Q is the corresponding diol with carboxylic acid and aldehyde groups. Options involve Lindlar's catalyst/H2, NaBH4, SnCl2/HCl, H3O+ in various sequences.

For a double strand DNA, one strand is given below: 5' A G T C A C G T A A G T C 3'. The amount of energy required to split the double strand DNA into two single strands is ___ kcal mol-1. [Given: Average energy per H-bond: A-T = 1.0 kcal/mol, G-C = 1.5 kcal/mol, A-U = 1.25 kcal/mol. Ignore electrostatic repulsion between phosphate groups.]

An organic compound P having molecular formula C6H6O3 gives ferric chloride test and does not have intramolecular hydrogen bond. Compound P reacts with 3 equivalents of NH2OH to produce oxime Q. Treatment of P with excess methyl iodide in the presence of KOH produces compound R as the major product. Reaction of R with excess iso-butylmagnesium bromide followed by treatment with H3O+ gives compound S as the major product. The total number of methyl (-CH3) groups in compound S is ___.

An organic compound P with molecular formula C9H18O2 decolorizes bromine water and also shows positive iodoform test. P on ozonolysis followed by treatment with H2O2 gives Q and R. While compound Q shows positive iodoform test, compound R does not give positive iodoform test. Q and R on oxidation with PCC followed by heating give S and T, respectively. Both S and T show positive iodoform test. Sum of number of oxygen atoms in S and T is ___.
Complete copolymerization of 500 moles of Q and 500 moles of R (from organic compound P with C9H18O2) gives one mole of a single acyclic copolymer U. The molecular weight of U is ___.

Consider the depicted hydrogen (H) in the hydrocarbons given below. The most acidic hydrogen (H) is [four cyclopentadiene/fulvene-type hydrocarbons with allylic/vinylic H atoms shown; options A-D show different substituted cyclopentadienyl structures]

For the reaction sequence given below, the correct statement(s) is(are): Cyclohexane + HBr -> P; P + Finkelstein reaction -> Q; P + Swarts reaction -> R. (X is any atom other than carbon and hydrogen, different in P, Q and R). Statements compare C-X bond length, enthalpy, SN2 reactivity, and pKa of leaving groups.

The monomer (X) involved in the synthesis of Nylon 6,6 gives positive carbylamine test. If 10 moles of X are analyzed using Dumas method, the amount (in grams) of nitrogen gas evolved is ______. Use: Atomic mass of N (in amu) = 14

The reaction sequence given below is carried out with 16 moles of X. The yield of the major product in each step is given below the product in parentheses. The amount (in grams) of S produced is ______. X = 2-bromophenyl 1,3-dioxolane (molar mass 210); (i) Na, dry ether (ii) H3O+ -> P (100%); (i) NaOH, Delta (ii) H3O+ -> Q (50%); (i) NaOH, CaO (ii) Delta -> R (50%); PBr3, (C2H5)2O -> T (50%); NaH, (C2H5)2O -> S (50%)

The major products obtained from the reactions in List-II are the reactants for the named reactions mentioned in List-I. Match each entry in List-I with the appropriate entry in List-II. List-I: (P) Stephen reaction; (Q) Sandmeyer reaction; (R) Hoffmann bromamide degradation reaction; (S) Cannizzaro reaction. List-II: reactions starting from Toluene, Benzoic acid, Nitrobenzene, Toluene, Aniline.

Match the compounds in List-I with the appropriate observations in List-II. List-I: (P) 4-hydroxyphenyl-alanine-glycine methyl ester dipeptide; (Q) N-acetyl-phenylalanine-valine methyl ester; (R) benzylammonium chloride (PhCH2NH3+Cl-); (S) 2,6-dimethylphenyl hydrazine (NHNH2). List-II: (1) azo dye; (2) ninhydrin purple + FeCl3 violet; (3) hydrazone with glucose; (4) blood red with FeCl3; (5) ninhydrin test no phthalein dye.

sub_topic: Multi-step Synthesis / Named Reactions (HVZ, Aldol, Grignard, Ozonolysis)
question_text: Monocyclic compounds P, Q, R and S are the major products formed in the reaction sequences given below. (1) PhCH2COOH + Br2/Red phosphorus, H2O -> P; (2) PhCHO + HCHO + aq. NaOH, 293K -> Q; (3) PhC-triple bond-CH + (i) NaNH2, allyl bromide, (ii) Hg2+, H3O+ -> R; (4) indene + (i) O3, Zn-H2O, (ii) CH3MgBr (2 equiv.), (iii) H+, Delta -> S. The product having the highest number of unsaturated carbon atom(s) is

The correct reaction/reaction sequence that would produce a dicarboxylic acid as the major product is: (A) HO-CH2-CH2-Cl + (i) NaCN, (ii) HO-, H2O, (iii) H3O+; (B) CHO-(CHOH)4-CH2OH + Br2, H2O; (C) bromocyclohexene + (i) KOH, EtOH, (ii) KMnO4, H2SO4, Delta; (D) 2-methyl-3-hydroxybutan-2-one + H2CrO4

For the reaction sequence given below, the correct statement(s) is(are): tetralin (1,2,3,4-tetrahydronaphthalene) + (i) KMnO4, H+, Delta, (ii) NH3, Delta, -2H2O -> X; X + (i) strong heating, (ii) ethanolic KOH, (iii) R-Br -> Y; Y + NaOH -> aromatic compound + Z. Statements about X, Y, Z.

For the reaction sequence given below, the correct statement(s) is(are): Ph-CH2-substituted delta-lactone + LiAlH4 -> P; P + H2SO4, 443K -> S; P + CrO3-H2SO4 -> Q; Q + NaOH and CaO, Delta -> R. Statements: (A) P is optically active. (B) S gives Bayer's test. (C) Q gives effervescence with aq. NaHCO3. (D) R is an alkyne.

A linear octasaccharide (molar mass = 1024 g mol-1) on complete hydrolysis produces three monosaccharides: ribose, 2-deoxyribose and glucose. The amount of 2-deoxyribose formed is 58.26% (w/w) of the total amount of the monosaccharides produced in the hydrolyzed products. The number of ribose unit(s) present in one molecule of octasaccharide is ______. Use: Molar mass (g mol-1): ribose = 150, 2-deoxyribose = 134, glucose = 180; Atomic mass (amu): H = 1, O = 16
